name: Iboga
description: Iboga (Tabernanthe iboga) is a sacred psychoactive plant central to the Bwiti religion practiced in Gabon, Cameroon, and other parts of West Central Africa. It is revered for its ability to induce profound spiritual experiences, facilitating communication with ancestors and the spirit world. The root bark of the iboga plant contains ibogaine, a naturally occurring psychoactive alkaloid. In Bwiti rituals, iboga is ingested in large doses to initiate individuals into the religion, heal physical and psychological ailments, and provide guidance and insight. The experience is often described as a journey through one's life, revealing past traumas and forgotten memories. Iboga is considered a powerful teacher and healer, capable of transforming lives and connecting individuals to their spiritual roots. The use of iboga is deeply intertwined with the cultural identity and social structure of the Bwiti people, serving as a cornerstone of their spiritual practice and worldview. The plant is treated with immense respect, and its use is carefully regulated by experienced practitioners. The ritual involves complex dances, songs, and prayers, all designed to create a sacred space for healing and transformation. The Bwiti believe that iboga allows them to access the collective unconscious and gain wisdom from their ancestors, fostering a sense of community and shared identity. Beyond its spiritual uses, iboga also holds significance in traditional medicine, used to treat various ailments, including addiction, fever, and fatigue. However, its primary importance lies in its ability to facilitate spiritual growth and connection to the divine.
subtitle: Sacred root for spiritual awakening.

keyMyths.title: The Discovery of Iboga
keyMyths.description: Legend says iboga was discovered by pygmy hunters who observed animals consuming the root before undertaking long journeys. They then experimented with the plant themselves, discovering its psychoactive properties.
keyMyths.source: Oral tradition of the Bwiti people

sources.author: De Loor, Cindy
sources.work: Iboga: The Root of African Wisdom
sources.description: An exploration of iboga's cultural and spiritual significance in Gabon.
sources.text: An exploration of iboga's cultural and spiritual significance in Gabon.

sources.author: Fernandez, James W.
sources.work: Bwiti: An Ethnography of the Religious Imagination in Africa
sources.description: A detailed anthropological study of the Bwiti religion and the role of iboga.
